explicit_text: I was born in 1945 in Xiangnan, which is part of Zieng Khouang in Laos. This place is where my early life began before the war started. Growing up there, I witnessed the struggles and conflicts that shaped my path as a soldier in the Secret War. Xiangnan holds a significant place in my memories as the starting point of my journey.

explicit_text: Xiangkhouang Province is where I was born and where much of the fighting took place during the Secret War. The Plain of Jars, a key battlefield, is located in this province. It was here that I fought alongside leaders like Colonel Nhia Lue and Lieutenant Colonel Vang Koua. Xiangkhouang was central to the conflict and to my experiences as a young soldier.

explicit_text: Laos is my homeland where I was born and where I fought in the Secret War against the communists. After the communists took over the country, life changed drastically, and I had to make the difficult decision to leave. Laos will always be part of my story, as it shaped my identity and the reasons I eventually came to the United States in 1979.

explicit_text: I was a young soldier, just 17 or 18 years old, when I joined the Secret War in Laos. We fought fiercely against the communist forces, especially in battles like the one at the Plain of Jars. I remember taking photos with fellow soldiers and leaders such as Colonel Nhia Lue and Lieutenant Colonel Vang Koua during these intense times. The war ended when the communists took over, forcing many of us to flee the country.
